question: In the situation shown in Figure, when vehicle A passes the point indicated by X, it must slow down.
description: Vehicle A doesn't have to slow down because it has the right of way.

question: When driving down a long downward slope, drivers of four-wheeled vehicles should mainly apply the foot brake and drivers of two-wheeled vehicles should mainly apply the brakes on the front and back wheels. Engine braking should be used as an auxiliary.
description: Drivers should use mainly engine braking and apply the foot brake as an auxiliary.

question: When parking or stopping a regular motor vehicle on a general road at night, even if the driver positions an emergency warning reflector device at the rear of the vehicle, he or she must turn on the hazard lights, parking lights, or tail lamps.
description: The hazard lights, parking lights, or tail lamps are not necessary when an emergency reflector device is positioned.

question: The owner of an automobile (excluding two-wheeled motor vehicles) must obtain a personal parking place for it that is not on a road and is within 5 kilometers of the address or other base position from which the automobile will be used.
description: The personal parking place must be within 2 km.

question: Parking and stopping are permitted in tunnels with vehicular lanes.
description: Neither parking nor stopping a vehicle is allowed in tunnels with and without vehicular lanes.

question: Where there is a traffic sign as shown in Figure, vehicles must not change lanes or pass on the side in order to overtake automobiles or general mopeds.
description: In a no overtaking zone, vehicles must not change lanes in order to overtake automobiles or general mopeds.

question: The method of passing around the curve as shown in Figure is correct.
description: Drivers should slow down before the curve and accelerate at the end of the curve.

question: When a vehicle attempts to overtake the vehicle ahead, the vehicle ahead may not notice and begin to change to the right lane. If the driver of the overtaking vehicle senses danger, he or she may sound the horn.
description: The horn can be used when needed to avoid danger.

question: Parking is prohibited within 5 meters of a fire hydrant, a place where firefighting equipment is stored, or a fire alarm.
description: Parking is prohibited within 1 meter of a fire alarm.

question: Vehicles other than large-size trucks loaded with cargo may not use the slower traffic lane of an expressway.
description: Any vehicles that cannot satisfy the minimum speed limit are allowed to use it.

question: Where there is a traffic sign as shown in Figure, vehicles loaded with cargo of a height of up to 3.3 meters from the cargo bed may proceed.
description: Only vehicles with a total aboveground height of up to 3.3 meters may proceed.

question: When there are passengers getting on and off a streetcar, a driver must stop until all of the passengers or other people finish crossing the road, regardless of whether there is a safety zone or not.
description: Vehicles may proceed at a low speed when passing a safety zone.

question: The traffic sign shown in Figure designates a national expressway or a motorway.
description: This is a sign designating motor vehicles only, and is placed on a national expressway or a motorway.

question: An automobile may stop for ten minutes on a road where there is less than 3.5 meters of space between the right side of the vehicle and the right side of the road while the driver stands beside the vehicle and gives instructions for the loading of cargo.
description: Drivers are allowed to park when loading or unloading cargo and the driver is immediately available to drive.

question: When tires wear down, the frictional resistance between the road surface and the tires decreases. Braking distance increases but reaction distance is not affected.
description: The reaction distance has nothing to do with worn tires because the brakes don't start to operate at the reaction distance.

question: When a vehicle collides with an object at rest, if it can decrease its speed to half, the kinetic energy at the time of impact will be just about one-quarter of that generated when speed is not deceased.
description: The kinetic energy at the time of a collision is proportional to the square of speed. 1/2 x 1/2 = 1/4.

question: The use of a child seat is not required for a preschool child less than six years of age riding in the rear seat of a four-wheeled vehicle.
description: The use of a child seat is required on the rear seat as well.

question: If loading cargo that cannot be divided as shown in Figure, a driver need not obtain a permit from the police chief having jurisdiction over the place of departure.
description: A permit must be obtained because the total length extends beyond more than 1/10th of the length behind the automobile.

question: The hydroplaning phenomenon, which occurs while driving at high speed, is the 'standing wave' phenomenon that occurs when tire pressure is low.
description: Wave shaped ripples on the tire are called the standing wave phenomenon.

question: The seating capacity noted on the motor vehicle inspection certificate does not include the driver.
description: The driver is included in the seating capacity.

question: When the hand brake of a four-wheeled vehicle is pulled all the way, there should not be any room to pull it further.
description: There should be room to pull it further. Otherwise the brake doesn't operate well.

question: Centrifugal force increases proportionally to the square of speed, and also increases as the radius of the curve decreases.
description: This is a law of nature (law of physics). One must be fully aware of it when driving on a curved road.

question: When there is enough passing clearance on the left side of the vehicle ahead, a vehicle may overtake the vehicle ahead by passing on the left side.
description: When overtaking, a vehicle, in principle, must pass on the right side of the vehicle overtaken.

question: If a vehicle’s center of gravity becomes high and it leans to one side it is likely to turn over, so cargo must be loaded as low as possible and distributed to the left and right rather than on one side.
description: The vehicle would not be stable unless the center of gravity is low and evenly balanced in the middle of the vehicle.

question: When towing a broken-down vehicle using a towing rope, the distance between vehicles must be 5 meters or less and a red cloth with an area of 0.3 square meters or more must be attached to the center of the rope.
description: Attach a white cloth not a red cloth.

question: When an oncoming vehicle is crossing the centerline to overtake another vehicle, a driver has no choice except to cut in front of a vehicle that is displaying a novice driver sign.
description: Such an action cannot be helped to avoid a head-on collision.

question: Correct driving posture enables reliable driving performance and reduces fatigue, so drivers should not relax their driving posture by tilting their body or driving with their elbow resting on the window ledge.
description: It is important to maintain a posture that allows the driver to correctly operate the steering wheel or pedals in case of an emergency.

question: Where there is a pavement marking as shown in Figure, vehicles are permitted to proceed but must not stop within it.
description: This marking designates a 'no stopping zone' where vehicles must not stop.

question: When a driver is being overtaken by another vehicle, it is not particularly necessary for the driver to give way in their lane, as long as he or she does not accelerate, and even if there is not enough room between his or her vehicle and the vehicle in front.
description: Drivers should give way to the overtaking vehicle by moving over to the left as much as possible.

question: If the pressure of the front right tire is lower than that of the front left tire, the steering wheel will pull to the left.
description: The steering wheel will pull to the right because it is pulled to the side with a lower pressure tire.

question: If a person applying for a renewal of license and equivalent is the age of 65 or over at the renewal period expiration date, that person must take a senior driver's course within six months of the expiration date.
description: A person who is the age of 70 or over must take a senior driver's course.

question: When overtaking, it is advisable for a driver to give a signal by turning on the direction indicator, then after about 3 seconds, change the lane while gradually accelerating.
description: A driver must give a signal 3 seconds before changing lanes, change the lane gradually and avoid swerving.

question: The traffic sign shown in Figure designates that vehicles may park if the road is at least 6 meters wide.
description: The sign designates that vehicles may not park unless they leave an open space of 6 meters or more to the right of the vehicle.

question: In the situation shown in Figure, vehicle A must not overtake vehicle B, even if it is safe ahead and to the rear.
description: Vehicle A may overtake vehicle B if it is safe because the road they are traveling on has right of way.

question: When the signal ahead is a red light and a green arrow pointing to the left at the same time, automobiles and general mopeds may proceed in the direction indicated by the arrow, but specified small mopeds and light vehicles may not proceed.
description: Specified small mopeds and light vehicles may also execute a left turn when the green arrow points to the left.

question: When checking the chain of a two-wheeled vehicle by pressing the middle of the chain with one's finger, there should be no slack and it should be tight.
description: The chain requires a proper amount of slack.

question: When depressing the brake pedal of a four-wheeled vehicle all the way, it is best if there is a little space between the pedal and the floorboard (pedal reserve distance).
description: The brake doesn't function well and it is dangerous when there is little space between the pedal and the floorboard.

question: When driving in fog, snowstorms or similar conditions, turn on fog lamps and headlights early, use the centerline, guard rail, and tail lamps of vehicles ahead as a guide, reduce speed, and if necessary sound the horn.
description: Invisibility is the most dangerous factor when driving. Pay the utmost attention to safety.

question: Carrying cargo in the cargo bed in such a manner that it obstructs a clear view of the vehicle's direction indicators, tail lamps, brake lights, license plate, etc. is permitted if a person to observe the cargo rides along.
description: Driving under such conditions is dangerous and should be avoided even if a person to observe the cargo rides along.

question: Daily checks include not only those performed when the vehicle is stopped, but also those performed during driving.
description: Brake effectiveness and engine performances are also checked during driving.

question: When leaving a main through lane, reduce speed sufficiently while in the main through lane before entering the deceleration lane.
description: It is dangerous to reduce speed too much in the main through lane. Reduce speed sufficiently after entering the deceleration lane.

question: When intending to overtake another vehicle, a vehicle should reduce the clearance between it and the vehicle to be overtaken as much as possible in order to avoid a head-on collision with an oncoming vehicle.
description: The driver must ensure that there is a safe passing clearance in order not to hit the side of the vehicle to be overtaken.

question: The frictional resistance between tires and the road surface has a strong relationship to reaction distance.
description: It has a relationship to braking distance but not to reaction distance.

question: On a snow-covered road, the wheel prints made by previous vehicles are extremely slick, so drivers must avoid the prints as much as possible.
description: It is safer and easier to drive on the ruts made by previous vehicles.

question: When there is a side strip designated by a single white line, a vehicle may enter the side strip to stop and park if there is 0.75 meters or more of space on the left side of the vehicle.
description: Vehicles may enter the side strip to stop or park only if it is wide enough to leave 0.75 meters or more space.

question: In the situation shown in Figure, vehicle A may cross to the right to a minimum extent and overtake vehicle B.
description: Vehicle A may not cross to the right in order to overtake vehicle B unless the width is less than 6 meters (e.g. 5.99 meters or less).

question: Overtaking is prohibited along steep downward slopes, but not steep upward slopes.
description: Overtaking is not prohibited along steep upward slopes because some vehicles are not able to maintain the required speed.

question: When passing around a curve it is advisable to reduce speed just before the curve and enter it slowly, and then gradually accelerate slightly before completing the turn around the curve.
description: This is a useful method for passing around a curve readily and safely.

question: When driving a regular motor vehicle, if the vehicle cannot be driven further on an expressway due to a breakdown, etc. the driver must place an emergency warning reflector device to the rear of the vehicle. At night (or during the day when visibility is 200 meters or less), in addition to putting out an emergency warning reflector device the driver must also turn on the hazard lights, parking lights, or tail lamps.
description: The driver must display something highly visible to other traffic approaching at a high speed.

question: The traffic sign shown in Figure designates a bicycle path or a dedicated road for bicycles.
description: This sign designates a bicycle crossing.

question: When overtaking, it is advisable for a driver to check the safety to the right and behind to the right using a rearview mirror, as well as to check the situation in front.
description: Overtaking is a dangerous driving operation. It is necessary to thoroughly check safety.

question: When checking tires, check the tire pressure and tread depth and make sure that there are no cracks or any other damage, nails, stones, or any other foreign objects lodged in the tire, or abnormal wear.
description: Checking these items is necessary because they may cause flat tires or influence the braking distance.

question: When driving an automobile past a safety zone, drivers must slow down whether or not pedestrians are present.
description: Drivers don't have to slow down unless pedestrians are present.

question: At night, when approaching intersections or curves with poor visibility, a driver should use the high beams or flash them to warn other drivers or pedestrians of the approach of the vehicle.
description: Drivers should effectively utilize the headlights at night or when visibility is poor.

question: Parking and stopping are prohibited near the top of an uphill slope or on a steep downhill slope, but not on a steep uphill slope.
description: Parking and stopping are prohibited on a steep uphill slope, too.

question: When passing oncoming traffic on narrow roads that have a cliff on one side with a dangerous drop, the vehicle on the side opposite from the cliff side should stop initially and allow the vehicle on the cliff side to go ahead.
description: The vehicle on the cliff side is in more danger. It should stop initially and allow the oncoming traffic to pass.

question: The two traffic signs shown in Figure express the same meaning for all vehicles moving in the direction facing the signs.
description: They are signs to designate 'closed to all vehicles' and 'no entry for vehicles' respectively, expressing the same meaning for all vehicles moving in the direction facing the signs.

question: In the situation shown in Figure, the regular motor vehicle must yield the road to the streetcar.
description: The vehicle on the wider street has right of way. If the cross street is the same width, the vehicle may not obstruct the progress of the streetcar.

question: When intending to overtake on a road with vehicular lanes, a driver must use the vehicular lane immediately to the right of the lane he or she is currently driving in.
description: Drivers should overtake in a manner not to disturb other traffic flows.

question: When turning the steering wheel while traveling around a corner or curve, if speed increases three times centrifugal force increases six times.
description: Centrifugal force is proportional to the square of speed. When speed increases three times, its centrifugal force increases nine times.

question: Changing lanes or moving alongside an automobile or general moped in order to overtake is prohibited at or within 30 meters before an intersection (excluding intersections of roads with right of way), railway crossing, pedestrian crossing, or bicycle crossing.
description: These areas are all extremely dangerous places. Overtaking is prohibited in these areas because it requires acceleration.

question: A passenger vehicle with a seating capacity of 5 people may carry, in addition to the driver, 1 adult and 4 children under 12 years of age.
description: This is within the seating capacity since three children under 12 years of age are counted as two adults.

question: The maximum permitted cargo height for regular motor vehicles (excluding three-wheeled vehicles and vehicles with an engine displacement of 660 cc or less) is, in principle, 3.8 meters from the cargo bed.
description: Maximum permitted cargo height is 3.8 meters from the ground, not from the cargo bed.

question: Drivers of four-wheeled vehicles perceive two-wheeled vehicles to be closer and moving at a faster speed than they actually are.
description: The distance to and speed of two-wheeled vehicles tend to be misjudged because they are small in size.

question: When making a right turn from a one-way street. an automobile must initially approach the center of the road and proceed inside the center point of the intersection while slowing down, (Except roundabout.)
description: In order to make a right turn from a one-way street, a driver should initially approach the right edge of the road.

question: When a vehicle's rear wheels slip sideways to the right, it is best to turn the steering wheel to the left.
description: The driver should turn the steering wheel to the right because it is necessary to turn it to the direction where the wheels slip sideways.

question: Stopping is permitted within 10 meters of the signboard marker post) of a bus stop or streetcar stop, even during business hours if it is to let passengers get in or out.
description: Stopping or parking is not permitted in this area during business hours. One should not stop even to let passengers get in and out of the vehicle

question: Vehicles do not need to slow down along a steep upward slope, but must slow down along a steep downward slope.
description: Vehicles must slow down along a steep downward slope where they are accelerated and the brakes do not work effectively.

question: Drinking alcohol causes one’s sense of judgment and alertness to diminish and one’s ability to drive deteriorates, so people must not drive after drinking alcohol, however little they drink.
description: Driving when intoxicated is the most dangerous. One must not drive a vehicle after drinking alcohol.

question: If a police officer is making the hand signal for “stop” at an intersection, vehicles should stop one meter before the police officer.
description: Drivers should stop immediately before the intersection.

question: At an intersection that has no traffic control as shown in Figure, the regular motor vehicle approaching from the left of the general moped has the right of way.
description: The wider street has the right of way, so the general moped may proceed through the intersection before the regular motor vehicle.

question: Throwing rubbish such as cigarette butts, paper scraps, or empty cans out of a vehicle is prohibited, as is putting any part of one’s body or any object outside a vehicle while it is moving.
description: A good driver should not be engaged in actions against public morals or that could be considered dangerous.

question: Even if there is danger of a head-on collision, vehicles must not leave the road.
description: One should not hesitate to leave the road to avoid a head-on collision as long as it is safe.

question: If a four-wheeled vehicle’s engine stalls or won’t start inside a railway crossing, intersection, or similar, the driver should move the vehicle by shifting to a low gear and using the starter motor without depressing the clutch pedal as an emergency measure. (This does not apply to automatic transmission vehicles or manual transmission vehicles equipped with a clutch start switch.)
description: It is best to perform this method at a railway crossing after notifying train drivers.

question: On parts of national expressways where main through lanes traveling in opposite directions are not separated by a physical divider, the maximum speed limit is the same as on general roads.
description: The speed limit is the same as on general roads because it is a dangerous place where there is no physical divider.

question: In the main through lanes of national expressways, the maximum speed limit for all regular motor vehicles is 100 km/h.
description: The maximum speed limit for three-wheeled vehicles and towing vehicles is 80 km/h.

question: When a vehicle slips on a rainy day after the brakes are applied, the driver should think that the brakes are not working properly and apply them forcefully once more.
description: This action is dangerous and may result in further skidding. The driver should avoid pressing the brake pedal forcefully.

question: When a police officer is holding both of his or her arms straight up vertically at an intersection, this has the same meaning as a red light for traffic crossing the path of traffic proceeding parallel to the stance of the police officer.
description: For the traffic proceeding towards the front or back of the police officer, this hand signal has the same meaning as a red light.

question: Overtaking is prohibited at a pedestrian crossing or bicycle crossing or within 30 meters before and after one.
description: Overtaking is prohibited within 30 meters before a pedestrian crossing or bicycle crossing, but not after one.

question: The traffic sign shown in Figure prohibits right turns for large-size trucks, specified medium vehicles, and special heavy equipment, so other vehicles may turn right.
description: Any trucks with a maximum load of 2 tons or more may not execute a right turn.

question: When there is a streetcar stoppec on the road, a vehicle may pass it if there are no passengers getting on or off the streetcar in a safety zone.
description: A vehicle must slow down in a safetv zone where a streetcar is stopped even when there are no passengers getting on or off in the zone

question: If a driver is unavoidably forced to park on an expressway because the vehicle cannot be driven due to a breakdown, etc., the driver must park on a sufficiently wide shoulder or side strip
description: Park the car in a place where it doesn't impede the progress of other vehicles
